title: Protocol for Preparing Brain Samples for MUSIC
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"4khlc","text":"Here states the detailed procedure to prepare brain samples for MUSIC study.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Cut a portion of post-mortem human brain frontal cortex sample collected from The Brain and Body Donation Program (BBDP) at Banner Sun Health Research Institute on dry ice with heavy razor blades, and collect 50 mg of the sample in a 1.5 mL LoBind tube.
Use Chromium Nuclei Isolation kit (10X genomics, 1000494) to isolate nuclei from crosslinked cortex samples.
Transfer 50 mg frozen tissue into pre-chilled sample dissociation tube.

Add 400 μL of Lysis Buffer to Sample Dissociation Tube. Dissociate tissue with plastic pestle until homogeneous. A B 1 Component Volume (µL) 2 Lysis Reagent 1000 3 Reducing Agent B 1 4 Sufactant A 10 5 Total Volume 1011 Lysis Buffer
Equally load the solution into two nuclei isolation column, and centrifuge the tubes at 16,000 x g for 20 sec at 4°C.
Vortex the flowthrough in the collection tube that contains nuclei for 10 sec at 3,200 rpm or max speed to resuspend nuclei.
Centrifuge the collection tubes for 3 min at 500 x g at 4°C to pellet nuclei. Carefully discard the supernatant.
Thaw the 50 mg of brain sample on ice, and chop the tissue into smaller pieces by pestle. Store the rest of the sample at -80°C.
Incubate the sample with 10 mL of 2 mM disuccinimidyl glutarate (DSG) in 1X PBS in a 15 mL LoBind tube at room temperature for 45 min with gentle rotation.
Wash once with 10 mL of 1X PBS by centrifugation at 1,000 x g for 4 min.
Resuspend the sample in 15 mL of 1X PBS containing 3% formaldehyde, and incubate for 10 min with a gentle rotation.
Quench the crosslinking reaction by the addition of 5 mL of 1.25 M glycine followed by an incubation of 5 min with a rotation.
Centrifuge the sample at 1,000 x g for 4 min, and wash the sample twice with ice-cold 1X PBS containing 0.3% BSA (wt/vol).
Add 600 µL of lysis buffer into the tube, and mix 10 times by pipetting. Incubate on ice for 10 min.

Resuspend the nuclei in 500 µL of Debris Removal Buffer provided by the kit by pipetting 15 times. A B 1 Component Volume (µL) 2 Debris Removal Reagent 500 3 Reducing Agent B 0.5 4 Total Volume 500.5 Debris Removal Buffer
Centrifuge the nuclei at 700 x g for 10 min at 4°C. Carefully discard the supernatant.
Resuspend the nuclei in 1 mL of Wash and Resuspension Buffer. A B 1 Component Volume (µL) 2 1X PBS 1750 3 10% BSA 200 4 RNase Inhibitor (40X) 50 5 Total Volume 2000 Wash and Resuspension Buffer
Centrifuge the nuclei at 500 x g for 5 min at 4°C. Carefully discard the supernatant.
Resuspend the nuclei again in 1 mL of Wash and Resuspension Buffer.
Centrifuge the nuclei at 500 x g for 5 min at 4°C. Carefully discard the supernatant as much as possible.
The nuclei are subjected to nuclei counting and the following procedures.
creator: Wenxin Zhao
